stem: Let \( A = \begin{bmatrix} [[a]] & [[b]] \\ [[b]] & [[a]] \end{bmatrix} \), a real symmetric matrix. What is its largest eigenvalue?
solution: For \( A = \begin{bmatrix} a & b \\ b & a \end{bmatrix} \) the eigenvectors are \( (1,1) \) and \( (1,-1) \), giving eigenvalues \( a+b \) and \( a-b \). Here these are \( [[a]]+[[b]] \) and \( [[a]]-[[b]] \); the larger is \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the eigenvalue \( a+b \), which is not the larger one when \( b<0 \).
This is the eigenvalue \( a-b \), which is not the larger one when \( b>0 \).
This is the shared diagonal entry, the average of the two eigenvalues, not the largest eigenvalue.
This doubles the diagonal entry rather than adding the correct offset \( |b| \).
This is the off-diagonal entry, not an eigenvalue of the matrix.
This is one less than the correct largest eigenvalue.
This is two more than the correct largest eigenvalue.

stem: Let \( A = \begin{bmatrix} [[aa]] & [[bb]] \\ [[bb]] & [[dd]] \end{bmatrix} \), a real symmetric matrix with unequal diagonal entries. What is its largest eigenvalue?
solution: The eigenvalues of a general \( 2\times2 \) symmetric matrix \( \begin{bmatrix} a & b \\ b & d \end{bmatrix} \) are \( \frac{a+d}{2} \pm \sqrt{\left(\frac{a-d}{2}\right)^2 + b^2} \). Here the midpoint is \( [[c]] \) and the radius is \( [[r]] \), so the eigenvalues are \( [[c]]+[[r]] \) and \( [[c]]-[[r]] \); the largest is \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is only the midpoint \( \frac{a+d}{2} \), not the largest eigenvalue.
This is the smallest eigenvalue, not the largest.
This is the trace \( a+d \), not a single eigenvalue.
This is the average of the diagonal entries only, ignoring the off-diagonal contribution.
This is just one diagonal entry, not an eigenvalue in general.
This is one more than the correct largest eigenvalue.
This is one less than the correct largest eigenvalue.

stem: Let \( A = \begin{bmatrix} [[aa]] & [[bb]] \\ [[bb]] & [[dd]] \end{bmatrix} \), a real symmetric matrix with eigenvalues \( \lambda_1 \ge \lambda_2 \). What is \( \lambda_1 - \lambda_2 \)?
solution: For a symmetric \( \begin{bmatrix} a & b \\ b & d \end{bmatrix} \), the eigenvalue spread is \( \lambda_1-\lambda_2 = \sqrt{(a-d)^2+4b^2} = [[answer]] \) (twice the discriminant radius).
distractor_rationales: This is just the difference of the diagonal entries, omitting the off-diagonal contribution under the square root.
This is the trace \( a+d \), not the spread between the two eigenvalues.
This doubles the off-diagonal entry directly instead of combining it with \( (a-d)^2 \) under a square root.
This forgets to take the square root of the discriminant.
This is one more than the correct spread.
This is two less than the correct spread.

stem: Let \( A = \begin{bmatrix} [[a]] & [[b]] \\ [[b]] & [[d]] \end{bmatrix} \), a real symmetric matrix with (real) eigenvalues \( \lambda_1 \) and \( \lambda_2 \). What is \( \lambda_1^2 + \lambda_2^2 \)?
solution: For a symmetric matrix, \( \lambda_1^2+\lambda_2^2 = \operatorname{tr}(A^2) \). Squaring \( A \) gives diagonal entries \( a^2+b^2 \) and \( b^2+d^2 \), so \( \operatorname{tr}(A^2) = a^2 + 2b^2 + d^2 = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This omits the off-diagonal entry's contribution \( 2b^2 \) entirely.
This squares the trace \( (a+d)^2 = \lambda_1^2+2\lambda_1\lambda_2+\lambda_2^2 \) instead of the sum of squares.
This only counts the off-diagonal contribution once instead of twice (it appears in both diagonal entries of \( A^2 \)).
This is the determinant \( ad-b^2 \), which equals \( \lambda_1\lambda_2 \), not \( \lambda_1^2+\lambda_2^2 \).
This is one more than the correct sum of squares.
This is two less than the correct sum of squares.

stem: Let \( A = \begin{bmatrix} [[a]] & [[b]] & 0 \\ [[b]] & [[a]] & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & [[e]] \end{bmatrix} \), a real symmetric block-diagonal matrix. What is its largest eigenvalue?
solution: The matrix is block-diagonal: the top-left \( 2\times2 \) symmetric block has eigenvalues \( [[a]]+[[b]] \) and \( [[a]]-[[b]] \), and the bottom-right \( 1\times1 \) block contributes the eigenvalue \( [[e]] \). The full set of eigenvalues of \( A \) is the union of these; the largest is \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is one eigenvalue of the top block, but not necessarily the overall largest once the scalar block's eigenvalue \( e \) is included.
This is the other eigenvalue of the top block, but not necessarily the overall largest.
This is the scalar block's eigenvalue, but not necessarily the overall largest.
This is just the shared diagonal entry of the top block, not one of its actual eigenvalues in general.
This is one more than the correct largest eigenvalue.
This is one less than the correct largest eigenvalue.
